key_insight: An open-source LLM pipeline can convert messy longitudinal liver disease notes into structured data at scale, enabling reconstruction of patient trajectories and risk patterns.
clinical_relevance: This could help researchers mine routine clinical records for earlier liver cancer risk signals in chronic liver disease populations and support better surveillance studies.
method: Manually annotated 507 reports from 30 patients across radiology, pathology, and transplant assessments.
Benchmarked four open-source LLMs against a regex baseline on 73 extraction tasks.
Tested constrained decoding versus unconstrained prompting before applying the final pipeline to 835 transplanted patients.
evidence_signal: Llama 3.3 70B achieved >90% accuracy on 59 of 73 tasks, and constrained decoding reached >99.9% format adherence across 5,590 prompt-output pairs.
watch_item: Results come from a single-center transplant cohort, so external validation is needed before broader use across institutions and note types.
paper_title: LLM-based reconstruction of longitudinal clinical trajectories in chronic liver disease.
